phaseGoals.Create/Scaffold: Create the sample workspace, pull down the sample files, and initialize the azd project shape needed for the rest of the run.
phaseGoals.Provision: Provision the Azure-side infrastructure and verify that the expected account, project, model, and related resources exist.
phaseGoals.Local Run: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
phaseGoals.Deploy: Package the sample, push the image if needed, and register or deploy the hosted agent so the remote endpoint exists.
phaseGoals.Remote Invoke: Call the remotely deployed sample the way a user or engineer would and verify the result, latency, and first-use behavior.
phaseGoals.Monitor: Inspect the deployed agent, capture its metadata, and confirm whether the runtime looks healthy from a monitoring perspective.
phaseGoals.Update & Redeploy: Make a visible code change, redeploy it, and verify that the new behavior appears on a fresh remote execution path.
phaseGoals.Cleanup: Remove provisioned resources so the next run starts from a clean state and does not leak infrastructure.

steps.objective: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
steps.summary: P03 Local Run complete. azd ai agent run started .NET echo server successfully (dotnet 10.0.201, port 8088). azd ai agent invoke --local failed due to known --endpoint flag CLI bug. Curl fallback to /invocations returned HTTP 200 with coherent response {"echo":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}. Overall: PASS with workaround. Logs at P03-local-run.log. No follow-up work identified.

⚠️ Note: Server ignored PORT=8094 env var and started on default port 8088. This is a known azd/template behavior — the PORT env var is not wired to ASPNETCORE_URLS in this .NET template.

dotnet version: 10.0.201 Status: ✅ PASS

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint http://localhost:8088 '{ "message": "Hello!" }'

Exit code: 1 Duration: 0s

Output:

ERROR: unknown flag: --endpoint

Classification: CLI bug — azd ai agent invoke --local does not recognize --endpoint flag. Status: ❌ FAIL

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "message": "Hello!" }'

Exit code: 0 Duration: <1s HTTP Status: 200

Output:

{"echo":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}

Response is non-empty and coherent — the echo agent correctly echoed back the message. Status: ✅ PASS

steps.summary: P05 Remote Invoke completed with FAIL status. azd ai agent invoke returned HTTP 404 ResourceNotFound — Azure AI Foundry subdomain routing failure. The deployed agent endpoint does not resolve. This is a platform infrastructure issue, not RBAC/auth. Evidence logged to P05-remote-invoke.log. No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
Pre-flight Check

  • HOL-3533 (P04: Deploy): status = done ✅

Hard prerequisite satisfied. Proceeding with P05: Remote Invoke.

Step: remote_invoke.azd_invoke_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-responses/src && azd ai agent invoke 'Hello!'

Agent:        echo-agent-responses-nonstreaming (remote)
Message:      "Hello!"
Session:      (new — server will assign)
Conversation: conv_c6dcff6c777ce98f00YbquLRxTOEIilUFhDeZ4VDnM26Ml7pvL

Trace ID: ac4e75c1-021f-4784-b59f-1a73983234c1

ERROR: POST https://ai-account-7pzozzz4ixf74.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-responses/agents/echo-agent-responses-nonstreaming/endpoint/protocols/openai/responses?api-version=2025-05-15-preview failed with HTTP 404: 404 Not Found
{"error":{"code":"ResourceNotFound","message": "Subdomain does not map to a resource."}}

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~8s Azure correlation IDs: Trace ID: ac4e75c1-021f-4784-b59f-1a73983234c1 Status: ❌ FAIL Classification: Platform/infrastructure issue — Azure AI Foundry subdomain routing failure. The deployed agent endpoint ai-account-7pzozzz4ixf74.services.ai.azure.com returned 404 with ResourceNotFound: Subdomain does not map to a resource. The deploy (HOL-3533) reported success, but the remote endpoint is not routing correctly.

This is NOT an identity/RBAC issue (404, not 401/403), so the Identity/RBAC Retry Rule does not apply.

azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint http://localhost:8088 '{"message": "Hello!"}'

Exit code: 1 Output: ERROR: unknown flag: --endpoint

Command (attempt 2):

azd ai agent invoke --local --port 8088 '{"message": "Hello!"}'

Exit code: 1 Output:

Target:       localhost:8088 (local)
Message:      "{\"message\": \"Hello!\"}"
ERROR: POST http://localhost:8088/responses failed with HTTP 404: 404 Not Found

Status: ❌ FAIL — azd ai agent invoke --local always POSTs to /responses, but this is a /invocations template. Known CLI bug — azd invoke does not support /invocations protocol.

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message":"Hello!"}'

Exit code: 0 Duration: ~1s Output:

{"echo":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}

Status: ✅ PASS — Response is non-empty and coherent. Echo agent correctly echoed back the input message.

Server stopped cleanly with Ctrl-C after invoke completed.

Substep: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt

ERROR: VNext agents are currently enabled and require a session ID for log streaming.

Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Also tried: azd ai agent monitor --type system --no-prompt --tail 50 — same error.

Status: ❌ FAIL Classification: CLI bug — azd ai agent monitor requires a --session flag for vnext agents, but no session exists yet because P06 runs before P07 (Invoke). The CLI provides no way to list container health/status without a prior invocation session. This is a known azd CLI limitation for vnext-enabled agents.

🐤 P03: Local Run — Echo Streaming (.NET, /invocations)

Starting execution. Prerequisite HOL-3515 is done ✅.

Plan: 1. Self-provision JIT SSH to pc.int 2. Open tmux window for azd ai agent run (port 8095) 3. Invoke locally with azd ai agent invoke --local 4. Fall back to curl if needed 5. Stop server and capture logs

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-streaming-dotnet-invocations/src && ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:8095 azd ai agent run

Starting agent on http://localhost:8088 (Ctrl+C to stop)

      Now listening on: http://[::]:8088
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
      Application started. Press Ctrl+C to shut down.

Notes: - First attempt with PORT=8095 failed — .NET ignores the PORT env var - Second attempt with ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:8095 — Kestrel overrode it and bound to port 8088 from IConfiguration - Server started successfully on port 8088 - Port 8088 was initially in use from another template; killed existing process with fuser -k 8088/tcp before retry

Status: ✅ Pass (server started and listened on port 8088)

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-streaming-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke --local '{"message": "Hello!"}'

ERROR: failed invoking event handlers for 'postdeploy', extension azure.ai.agents project hook postdeploy failed: agent identity RBAC setup failed: AzureDeveloperCLICredential: please run "azd auth login"

Exit code: non-zero (due to postdeploy RBAC hook failure) Duration: ~15 minutes (container build + push + deploy)

Deploy itself: PASSED

The core azd deploy succeeded — container was built, pushed, agent was created (version 16), and environment variables were registered.

Postdeploy hook: FAILED (known issue)

The postdeploy RBAC hook failed with AzureDeveloperCLICredential auth error. This is a known azd deploy postdeploy auth defect — the RBAC hook uses AzureDeveloperCLICredential which may not be authenticated in the tmux bridge session. The deploy itself succeeded.

Azure correlation IDs: None visible in output. Classification: CLI bug (postdeploy hook auth credential issue)

steps.objective: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
steps.summary: P03 Local Run completed. azd ai agent run failed (pip not in PATH — CLI bug), workaround: manual pip install + python3 main.py. azd ai agent invoke --local failed (--endpoint flag not recognized — CLI bug), workaround: direct curl to /invocations. curl fallback PASSED with correct SSE streaming echo response. Agent is functional. No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Workaround was needed
Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-streaming-py-invocations/src && PORT=8089 azd ai agent run

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~2s Status: ❌ FAILED

Output:

Using startup command: python main.py
Warning: uv is not installed. Install it from https://docs.astral.sh/uv/
Falling back to pip...
Installing dependencies (requirements.txt)...

ERROR: failed to install dependencies: pip install failed: exec: "pip": executable file not found in $PATH

Classification: CLI bug — azd ai agent run requires pip or uv in PATH but the host only has python3 -m pip (externally-managed Python 3.12 on Ubuntu).

Workaround: Installed deps manually via python3 -m pip install --break-system-packages --user -r requirements.txt, then ran PORT=8089 python3 main.py directly.

Workaround result: ✅ Server started successfully on port 8089 within 2 seconds:

2026-04-14 16:41:01,498 INFO azure.ai.agentserver: AgentServerHost starting on 0.0.0.0:8089
[2026-04-14 16:41:01 -0600] [21004] [INFO] Running on http://0.0.0.0:8089 (CTRL + C to quit)

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-streaming-py-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint http://localhost:8089 '{ "message": "Hello!" }'

Exit code: 1 Duration: <1s Status: ❌ FAILED

Output:

ERROR: unknown flag: --endpoint

Classification: CLI bug — azd ai agent invoke --local does not accept --endpoint flag in the installed azd version.

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Command:

curl -s -N -X POST http://localhost:8089/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "message": "Hello!" }'

Exit code: 0 Duration: ~0.3s Status: ✅ PASSED

Output (SSE stream):

data: {"token": "🔊"}

data: {"token": "Echo:"}

data: {"token": "Hello!"}

event: done
data: {"invocation_id": "fcf2074c-c39c-42d2-b0d6-42147bba8000"}

Analysis: Response is non-empty, coherent, correctly echoes the input message with the 🔊 Echo: prefix. SSE streaming format is correct with proper data: and event: done framing. The agent is functioning as expected.

steps.html:
Pre-flight Check

✅ Prerequisite HOL-3469 (P04: Deploy) — status: done

Proceeding with P06: Monitor execution.

Plan

  1. Self-provision JIT SSH credentials for pc.int
  2. Connect to pc.int via jit_connect.py
  3. Run azd env get-values to discover AGENT_NAME and AGENT_VERSION
  4. Execute azd ai agent show (monitor.azd_show_attempt)
  5. Execute azd ai agent monitor (monitor.azd_monitor_attempt)
  6. Log all output to P06-monitor.log
  7. Post structured JSON artifact

Step 1: azd env get-values

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-streaming-py-invocations/src && azd env get-values

Exit code: 0 | Duration: ~3s

Key values discovered: - AGENT_ECHO_AGENT_INVOCATIONS_STREAMING_NAME="echo-agent-invocations-streaming" - AGENT_ECHO_AGENT_INVOCATIONS_STREAMING_VERSION=8 - AZURE_ENV_NAME="echo-streaming-py-invocations" - AZURE_LOCATION="northcentralus"

    "memory": "0.5Gi"
  }
}

Exit code: 0 | Duration: ~4s Status: ✅ Pass — agent metadata verified, version 8 deployed

Step 3: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt — azd ai agent monitor

Monitor requires a session ID for VNext agents. Invoked agent first to create a session, then streamed session logs.

3a: azd ai agent invoke (session creation)

azd ai agent invoke --no-prompt 'health check\'

Output:

Agent:    echo-agent-invocations-streaming (remote, invocations protocol)
Input:    "health check"
Session:  5605341563fefb3400C5zG0tTZAIGWEehancQ71FXbCXqEQBRB
Trace ID: 75d587ba-2c94-4e1d-a464-c5f91564cc68
ERROR: POST ...invocations?... failed with HTTP 424: 424 Failed Dependency
{"error":{"code":"internal_error","message":"Internal server error"}}

Exit code: 1 | Duration: ~5s Note: Session created successfully but invoke returned 424. This is the known Python /invocations request-body consumption bug — the server receives an empty body on the second read.

3b: azd ai agent monitor

azd ai agent monitor --session 5605341563fefb3400C5zG0tTZAIGWEehancQ71FXbCXqEQBRB --tail 100 --no-prompt

Key log output:

Session state: Running
AgentServerHost starting on 0.0.0.0:8088
AgentServerHost started
GET /readiness 200 (health probe passing)
POST /invocations 200 (first invocation OK)
ERROR: json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
POST /invocations 500 (second invocation — request body consumption bug)

Exit code: 0 | Duration: ~6s Status: ✅ Pass — monitor command succeeded, logs streaming, container healthy Azure correlation ID (trace): 75d587ba-2c94-4e1d-a464-c5f91564cc68

Known issue

The /invocations endpoint returns 500 on the platform-relayed invoke because the request body is consumed by the framework before reaching the handler. This is a known sample/SDK bug (Python request.json() reads empty body on second await).

Using startup command: python main.py

ERROR: failed to install dependencies: pip install failed: exec: "pip": executable file not found in $PATH

Status: ❌ FAIL — pip not in PATH. azd ai agent run cannot install dependencies. Classification: CLI bug / environment issue — azd looks for bare pip but system only has python3 -m pip.

Workaround: Manual venv + direct python3 run

steps.summary: P04 Deploy failed. Root cause: The original P02 provision ARM deployment "ai-project" is stuck in Running state for 1+ hour (Azure correlationId: 44cda4ac4d3b1f5fd131a832bbaaa622). The CognitiveServices/accounts resource never completed provisioning, so no AI project was created and AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ID was never set. Deploy cannot proceed without the project. Re-provision is blocked by the active deployment. Classification: infrastructure issue (stuck Azure ARM deployment). No follow-up work identified — this is a cascade from the incomplete provision and downstream phases will also fail.

steps.html:
P04 Deploy — Step 1: Pre-flight Check

Prerequisites verified: - HOL-3483 (P02: Provision): done ✅ - HOL-3484 (P03: Local Run): done ✅

Step 2: Initial Deploy Attempt

Command:

azd deploy --no-prompt

Output:

Deploying services (azd deploy)
Deploying service echo-agent-responses-streaming
Packaging service echo-agent-responses-streaming (Packaging container)
  (x) Failed: Deploying service echo-agent-responses-streaming

ERROR: Microsoft Foundry project ID is required: AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ID is not set
Suggestion: run azd provision or connect to an existing project

Exit code: 1 Status: FAILED — AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ID not set in azd environment

Root cause investigation: - azd env get-values shows only basic env vars (subscription, location, tenant) - No AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ID, AZURE_AI_FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ID, or AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT present - az resource list --resource-group rg-echo-streaming-py-responses shows only 3 resources: CognitiveServices account, Log Analytics, App Insights — no project exists - Comparing to successful template (multiturn-py-responses), the provision should have created an AI project under the account - P02 provision log is only 19 lines — truncated/incomplete, suggesting provision was interrupted

Step 3: Re-provision Attempt

Command:

azd provision --no-prompt

Output:

(x) Failed: Validating deployment

ERROR: deployment failed: DeploymentActive
The deployment with resource id .../deployments/ai-project cannot be saved,
because this would overwrite an existing deployment which is still active.
The previous deployment was started at 4/14/2026 10:21:10 PM with
correlationId 44cda4ac4d3b1f5fd131a832bbaaa622

Root cause: The original provision ARM deployment ai-project is stuck in Running state for over 1 hour. The Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts resource operation has been running for PT1H2M29S with statusCode Created but never completed.

Classification: Infrastructure issue — stuck Azure ARM deployment prevents project creation and blocks deploy.

This is not recoverable without either waiting for the stuck deployment to time out (expires 4/21) or manually canceling it in Azure Portal.

steps.html:
Pre-flight Check

✅ Hard prerequisite HOL-3485 (P04: Deploy) status: done

Proceeding with P05: Remote Invoke for Echo Streaming (Python, /responses).

Self-provisioning JIT SSH credentials now.

P05 Execution Journal — Step 1: Remote Invoke Attempt

Command

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/echo-streaming-py-responses/src
azd ai agent invoke echo-agent-responses-streaming "Hello!"

Output

ERROR: AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT not found in azd environment 'echo-streaming-py-responses'

Provide --account-name and --project-name flags, or run 'azd ai agent init' to configure the endpoint

Exit code: 1 Duration: <1s Azure correlation IDs: none (invoke never reached Azure) Status: FAILED

Root Cause

This is a cascade failure from P04 (HOL-3485). P04 was marked done but its artifact status is fail: - The original P02 provision ARM deployment ai-project was stuck in Running state for 1+ hour - The CognitiveServices/accounts resource was created but the AI project was never provisioned - AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ID and AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT were never set in the azd environment - Deploy (P04) failed because no project exists - Therefore there is no deployed agent to invoke

Resource Verification

az resource list --resource-group rg-echo-streaming-py-responses

Only 3 resources exist: CognitiveServices account, Log Analytics workspace, App Insights. No AI project, no deployed container.

Classification: Infrastructure issue — cascade from stuck Azure ARM deployment in P02 provision

steps.summary: P04 Deploy completed with FAIL status. Two deploy attempts both failed with identical error: azd remote build fails with archive/tar: write too long due to .venv (193M) in build context — known LangGraph Calculator template structural bug (missing .dockerignore). No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Issue or bug was called out
Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
Pre-flight Check

  • HOL-3499 (hard dep): ✅ done
  • HOL-3500 (soft dep): ✅ done

Proceeding with P04 Deploy. Self-provisioning JIT SSH credentials...

Step 1: azd deploy --no-prompt (attempt 1)

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/langgraph-calculator-py-responses/src && azd deploy --no-prompt

Output:

Deploying services (azd deploy)

Deploying service CalculatorAgent
Packaging service CalculatorAgent (Packaging container)
Publishing service CalculatorAgent (Publishing container)
  (x) Failed: Deploying service CalculatorAgent

ERROR: failed publishing service 'CalculatorAgent': container publish failed: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = remote build failed: archive/tar: write too long

Local fallback unavailable: the docker service is not running

Exit code: 1 (azd returned non-zero) Duration: ~20s Status: ❌ FAILED Classification: template issue — .venv (193M) from P03 Local Run included in Docker build context, no .dockerignore present

Step 2: azd deploy --no-prompt (retry 1)

Same command, same failure. Deterministic — the .venv bloat causes archive/tar: write too long on every attempt.

Root cause: LangGraph Calculator template creates a .venv during P03 Local Run (193M). The Dockerfile/build context has no .dockerignore to exclude it. The remote build (ACR) fails with archive/tar: write too long when trying to upload this bloated context.

This is a known template structural bug — the LangGraph Calculator scaffold does not produce a .dockerignore.

P04 Deploy Result — LangGraph Calculator (Python, /responses)

Status: ❌ FAIL

Root cause: Template structural bug — LangGraph Calculator scaffold does not include .dockerignore. P03 Local Run creates a .venv (193M) which gets included in the Docker build context. The ACR remote build then fails with archive/tar: write too long. Docker is not available locally on pc.int for local fallback.

This is a known template bug — the same failure pattern as documented in known-bugs.yaml for LangGraph Calculator provision/deploy failures.

Two attempts were made, both produced identical deterministic failure.

steps.html:
P03 Local Run — Multi-Turn Chat (.NET, /invocations)

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command: export AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=gpt-4o-mini && PORT=8096 azd ai agent run Exit code: 0 (server started successfully on port 8088 — azd overrides PORT) Note: First attempt failed with AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME not set. Workaround: export the env var manually from AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT value. Status: PASS (with workaround)

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Turn 1 Command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}' Turn 1 Response: "Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is a beautiful color. Do you have a favorite shade of blue?" Turn 1 session_id: 13479014-09ff-4689-83f4-d49a4bc3da73 Turn 1 Status: PASS — correctly acknowledges name and color.

Turn 2 Command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"message": "What is my name and what is my favorite color?", "session_id": "13479014-09ff-4689-83f4-d49a4bc3da73"}' Turn 2 Response: "I'm sorry, but I don't have access to personal information about you, including your name or favorite color." Turn 2 session_id returned: 3678631a-64d2-4606-ab14-270ab8ac77c2 (NEW — different from Turn 1) Turn 2 turn counter: 1 (reset, not 2) Turn 2 Status: FAIL — server did not preserve session context. Returned a new session_id and turn=1 despite passing the Turn 1 session_id.

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Used curl fallback for both turns since azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint was not used (curl is the documented fallback path for /invocations protocol).

Classification

Failure type: Template/sample bug — the .NET multiturn-chat-invocations sample does not properly persist session state across turns when session_id is passed in the request body.

Overall P03 Result: FAIL

Second response does not remember name or color from first turn.

azd ai agent monitor --tail 50 --no-prompt
azd ai agent monitor --type system --tail 50 --no-prompt

Exit code: 0 (both) Duration: ~5s each Status: ⚠️ SKIPPED (CLI limitation)

Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Classification: CLI bug / limitation — azd ai agent monitor does not support VNext agents without an active session ID. The agent is deployed and healthy per agent show, but the monitor command cannot retrieve logs without first creating a session via azd ai agent invoke. This is a known azd CLI gap for VNext hosted agents.

steps.objective: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
steps.summary: P03 Local Run PASS. Multi-turn state persistence verified: Turn 1 established context (name=Alice, color=blue), Turn 2 correctly recalled both. Workaround needed: AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME had to be manually set to gpt-4o (not auto-populated by azd env). PORT env var ignored by azd ai agent run (always binds to 8088). --endpoint flag not supported by azd ai agent invoke. No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
P03 Execution Journal — Local Run: Multi-Turn Chat (.NET, /responses)

Prerequisite Check

  • HOL-3547: done ✅

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-dotnet-responses/src && eval "$(azd env get-values | grep -E "^[A-Z_]+=.*" | sed "s/^/export /")" && export AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=gpt-4o && azd ai agent run

First attempt failed — AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME was not set by default:

Warning: AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME not set. Set it to your model deployment name.
Unhandled exception. System.ArgumentException: Value cannot be an empty string. (Parameter 'model')

Exit code: 134

Second attempt with explicit export AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=gpt-4o:

Using startup command: dotnet run
Starting agent on http://localhost:8088 (Ctrl+C to stop)
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[14]
      Now listening on: http://[::]:8088
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
      Application started. Press Ctrl+C to shut down.

Status: ✅ PASS (server started successfully on retry with env var set)

Note: PORT=8099 env var was ignored by azd ai agent run — server always binds to 8088. This is a known azd behavior.

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Turn 1 — Establish context:

azd ai agent invoke --local "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."

Output:

Target:       localhost:8088 (local)
Message:      "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."
Session:      f3f230a0-e8e7-48cd-ad5e-6e293640af1d
Conversation: 56054d62-cc30-4e6f-bd2a-29194fed95ed

[local] Hi, Alice! That's great to know—blue is such a calm and beautiful color. Let me know if there's anything you'd like to talk about! 😊

Exit code: 0 ✅ — Response acknowledges name and color.

Turn 2 — Test state persistence (no --new-session):

azd ai agent invoke --local "What is my name and what is my favorite color?"

Output:

Target:       localhost:8088 (local)
Message:      "What is my name and what is my favorite color?"
Session:      f3f230a0-e8e7-48cd-ad5e-6e293640af1d
Conversation: 56054d62-cc30-4e6f-bd2a-29194fed95ed

[local] Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue. 😊

Exit code: 0 ✅ — Second response correctly recalls BOTH name (Alice) and color (blue).

Status: ✅ PASS

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Not needed — azd ai agent invoke --local worked successfully.

Notes

  • --endpoint flag is not supported by current azd ai agent invoke. Default localhost:8088 was used.
  • PORT env var is ignored by azd ai agent run; server always binds to 8088.
  • AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME is NOT auto-populated by azd env get-values for this template — had to set it manually to gpt-4o.
  • Server stopped cleanly via Ctrl-C after invocations.

P03 Final Result — PASS ✅

Step 3: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt

Command:

azd ai agent monitor --no-prompt --tail 50

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~3s Status: ❌ FAIL (CLI limitation)

Error:

ERROR: VNext agents are currently enabled and require a session ID for log streaming.
Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Also tried --type system — same error. This is a known CLI limitation: azd ai agent monitor for VNext agents requires a session ID, which only exists after azd ai agent invoke. Without a prior invoke in this run, no session exists to stream logs from.

Classification: CLI bug / platform limitation

steps.summary: P03 Local Run completed. Result: FAIL. azd ai agent run started successfully (canonical pass). azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint failed (unknown flag). Curl fallback: Turn 1 passed (acknowledged Alice + blue), Turn 2 failed (session_id ignored, new session created, no state persistence). Root cause: known Python /invocations sample bug — session_id not consumed from request body. No follow-up work identified.

steps.html:
P03 Execution Started

Prerequisite check: HOL-3475 (P02: Provision) — ✅ done

Proceeding with P03: Local Run — Multi-Turn Chat (Python, /invocations)

Self-provisioning JIT SSH credentials...

P03 Execution Journal

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-py-invocations/src && AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=gpt-4o-mini PORT=8090 azd ai agent run

Exit code: 0 (server started, stopped with Ctrl-C after test) Duration: ~5s to start Output:

Using startup command: python main.py
Installing dependencies (requirements.txt)...
  ✓ Dependencies installed (requirements.txt)
Starting agent on http://localhost:8088 (Ctrl+C to stop)
Running on http://0.0.0.0:8088

Notes: - Server ignores PORT env var — always binds to 8088 - AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME must be explicitly set (not auto-populated from azd env). First run without it resulted in: Error code: 400 - {"error": {"message": "Missed model deployment"}} - Status: ✅ PASS — server started successfully

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command (canonical):

azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint http://localhost:8088 '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}'

Exit code: 1 Output: ERROR: unknown flag: --endpoint Status: ❌ FAIL — --endpoint flag not supported by current azd version

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Turn 1 command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."}'

Response (full_text): "Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is a beautiful color. Do you have a favorite shade of blue?" Session ID: e4efe37e-e0bc-4550-942b-291adda6fb42, Turn: 1 Status: ✅ PASS — correctly acknowledges name and color

Turn 2 command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message": "What is my name and what is my favorite color?", "session_id": "e4efe37e-e0bc-4550-942b-291adda6fb42"}'

Response (full_text): "I don't have access to personal information about you unless you've shared it in our conversation. If you tell me your name and favorite color, I can remember them for the duration of our chat!" Session ID: 7c8d392c-6bad-415a-9110-e2d41b355775 (DIFFERENT — session_id field was ignored), Turn: 1 (reset) Status: ❌ FAIL — does not recall name (Alice) or color (blue)

Root Cause

The Python /invocations sample does not honor the session_id field in the request body. A new session is created for each request, so multi-turn state is never persisted. This is a known template/sample bug (Python /invocations request-body consumption issue).

Overall P03 Result: ❌ FAIL

Multi-turn state persistence is broken. Classification: template/sample bug.

rootCauses: The Python `/invocations` sample does not honor the `session_id` field in the request body. A new session is created for each request, so multi-turn state is never persisted. This is a **known template/sample bug** (Python /invocations request-body consumption issue).

steps.html:
P03 Local Run — Multi-Turn Chat (Python, /responses)

Pre-flight

  • HOL-3491 (P02 Provision): done

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-py-responses/src && PORT=8092 azd ai agent run

Status: Server started successfully - Server launched on http://0.0.0.0:8088 (azd ignores PORT env for Python samples) - Dependencies installed, aiohttp fixed (required manual install of aiohttp 3.13.5 + legacy-cgi into venv) - Azure CLI credential acquired successfully

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command (canonical):

azd ai agent invoke --local --endpoint http://localhost:8088

Status: FAILED - --endpoint flag not supported (ERROR: unknown flag: --endpoint)

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Turn 1: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/responses with input "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue." Result: {"status":"failed","error":{"code":"server_error","message":"An internal server error occurred."}}

Turn 2: Same endpoint with previous_response_id Result: Same server_error

Root Cause

Server logs: FoundryApiError: Foundry storage request failed with HTTP 403

The multi-turn handler calls context.get_history() which hits the Foundry storage API and gets HTTP 403. The Azure CLI credential authenticates but lacks RBAC on the Foundry project storage for local runs.

Failure type: Platform / RBAC issue Status: FAIL

steps.summary: P05 Remote Invoke completed with FAIL status. Two layered failures: (1) azd ai agent invoke targets .services.ai.azure.com which returns 404 Subdomain does not map to a resource — platform routing defect. (2) Workaround via .cognitiveservices.azure.com reaches the hosted agent but returns server_error — RBAC/identity failure from postdeploy hook issue in P04. All results logged to P05-remote-invoke.log with structured artifact posted. No follow-up work identified — matches known platform/RBAC defect pattern.

steps.html:
P05 Execution Journal — Step 1: Pre-flight & Canonical Command

Pre-flight Check

  • HOL-3493 (P04 Deploy): done ✅
  • JIT SSH provisioned successfully (issuance 20f9de9a)
  • Working directory verified: /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-py-responses/src/
  • azd environment values loaded — agent endpoint: https://ai-account-nvtjqilzfsqwm.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-multiturn-py-responses/agents/multiturn-chat-responses/versions/17

Canonical Command: azd ai agent invoke

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-py-responses/src && azd ai agent invoke "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."

Exit code: 1

Output:

Agent:        multiturn-chat-responses (remote)
Message:      "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue."
Session:      (new — server will assign)
Conversation: conv_ac0ec1294cc65d4500u2LJUqCoThrsyjx1AfzBr14oZJ5dFTHa

Trace ID: 7ffecfcb-0255-47cd-b8cd-160d52bd2135

ERROR: POST https://ai-account-nvtjqilzfsqwm.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-multiturn-py-responses/agents/multiturn-chat-responses/endpoint/protocols/openai/responses?api-version=2025-05-15-preview failed with HTTP 404: 404 Not Found
{"error":{"code":"ResourceNotFound","message": "Subdomain does not map to a resource."}}

Classification: Platform issue — .services.ai.azure.com subdomain routing returns 404 "Subdomain does not map to a resource" even though the Azure resource exists (az cognitiveservices account show returns provisioningState: Succeeded).

Status: ❌ FAIL

Retry: Command retried once with same result.

P05 Execution Journal — Step 2: Workaround Attempt

Since azd ai agent invoke uses .services.ai.azure.com which returns 404, attempted curl workaround via .cognitiveservices.azure.com endpoint.

Diagnostic: Endpoint Probing

Endpoint Domain Token Audience HTTP Code Result
.services.ai.azure.com cognitiveservices 404 "Subdomain does not map to a resource"
.services.ai.azure.com ai.azure.com 404 "Subdomain does not map to a resource"
.cognitiveservices.azure.com cognitiveservices 403 Empty body (RBAC failure)
.cognitiveservices.azure.com ai.azure.com 200 server_error: An internal server error occurred

Workaround Command (curl via .cognitiveservices.azure.com)

Response (invoke 1):

{"id":"caresp_c2efc4081d5c767a0004EwX7u4lOhVMvofMXof5QiO8ffX82GG","status":"failed","error":{"code":"server_error","message":"An internal server error occurred."},"agent_session_id":"25b491d5..."}

Latency: 7550ms

Retry after 30s (invoke 1 retry):

{"id":"caresp_3311c9732651588000u6KuI5VRhA6dHcIgW5dTNglLaXQdzmlq","status":"failed","error":{"code":"server_error","message":"An internal server error occurred."}}

Latency: 7057ms

Response (invoke 2):

{"id":"caresp_608088ba9dd37c98009acpuSoZBWn2TP6hRsac5nmpnGQJbUzM","status":"failed","error":{"code":"server_error","message":"An internal server error occurred."}}

Latency: 7009ms

Root Cause

Two layered failures: 1. .services.ai.azure.com 404: azd cli sends invoke to this domain, but it returns "Subdomain does not map to a resource" — a platform routing defect. 2. Hosted agent server_error: When bypassing via .cognitiveservices.azure.com, the hosted agent container itself returns server_error. This is likely caused by the postdeploy RBAC hook failure noted in P04 — the agent container lacks the RBAC assignments needed to call the backing OpenAI endpoint.

Classification: Platform issue (subdomain routing) + RBAC/identity issue (postdeploy hook failure)

Log written to: /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-py-responses/logs/P05-remote-invoke.log

Substep: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt

Command:

azd ai agent monitor --tail 50 --no-prompt

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~2s Status: ⚠️ EXPECTED FAILURE (known CLI limitation)

Output:

ERROR: VNext agents are currently enabled and require a session ID for log streaming.
Suggestion: Specify the session ID using --session, or run `azd ai agent invoke` first to create one

Classification: CLI bug / VNext limitation — azd ai agent monitor requires --session for VNext hosted agents and cannot show general container health without a prior invoke session. This is a known azd CLI gap for VNext agents.

Also attempted --type system — same error.

Log file written: /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-14-rerun/multiturn-py-responses/logs/P06-monitor.log
